Class ButtonView

java.lang.Object
scorefour.view.ButtonView
All Implemented Interfaces:
Viewable
public class ButtonView extends Object implements Viewable
ButtonView initializes the visual aspects of a button and provides a way to draw these graphics to a Panel and edit the state of a graphic using the setIndex method.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    ButtonView(Rectangle bounds, int row)
    Constructs a ButtonView object which stores a pair of images which are defined by the row integer passed to it.

    Description
    void
    draw(Graphics g)
    The draw method is accessed by a Panel class to be drawn on a Frame

    setIndex(int index)
    ButtonView is made up of two images, hover and not hover, which can be switched between using the setIndex method.
    void

    Changes the button file images to be displayed by the ButtonView.

  • Constructor Details

    • ButtonView

      public ButtonView(Rectangle bounds, int row)
      Constructs a ButtonView object which stores a pair of images which are defined by the row integer passed to it.

      ButtonView is made up of two images, hover and not hover, which can be switched between using the setIndex method.

      The bounds dictate the size which the images will take up when drawn on the Panel.

      Parameters:
      bounds - a Rectangle object defining the size of the visual button
      row - the type of button to be displayed

  • Method Details

    • setIndex

      public void setIndex(int index)
      ButtonView is made up of two images, hover and not hover, which can be switched between using the setIndex method.
      Parameters:
      index - switch between not hover (0) and hover (1)

    • setRow

      public void setRow(int row)
      Changes the button file images to be displayed by the ButtonView.
      Parameters:
      row - the type of button to be displayed

    • draw

      public void draw(Graphics g)
      The draw method is accessed by a Panel class to be drawn on a Frame
      Specified by:
      draw in interface Viewable
      Parameters:
      g - the Graphics context used for rendering
